title: seriphium scabrum
intro: Seriphium scabrum, also known as prickly rosemary, is a shrub-like plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as indigestion, bloating, and gas, due to its ability to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut health. The plant's health benefits include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, reducing the risk of infection and promoting wound healing. Seriphium scabrum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried herb for use in various applications, including topical treatments and internal remedies.
